title: Cool new industry - Installing solar panels on houses

transcript.text: Alright, Economic Recovery Plan with Kyle. I just did a video where there's a new industry that we should have where we put, we place grass gardens with rock gardens.

And I also say another one you can do is installing solar panels on houses. Solar panel technology is increasing, so I have not researched that, but I think we are at this point now where the solar panels are good enough, where it makes sense to put solar panels on your house.

I was a window washer last year, and I did a house, somebody's house, and they showed me their solar panels. It was all over the roof of their house, a normal, modernly built house in the suburb. And they said it costed them like $9,000. There was a company that did it, there's a couple country companies in the front range that do it, and they said that they would make their money that they spent on it back within 10 years or something like this or something like that.

So yeah, you make your money back. So in the long run, it makes you money. Same thing with the rock garden thing. And also along with the rock garden thing, it's fun outside work. That's good. Exercise. Along with the rock garden work thing.

It's also something where people can work for themselves. Small companies work for themselves installing, revamping the infrastructure. And to, you know, get the people to help pay for that to happen. They can maybe get, you know, bank loans or whatever.

But like I said with the rock garden idea, that there's always people with money. So you want to go after those people first. The people with money, who have enough money to spend on that kind of thing.
And then those people do it, and then they can, you know, they can go, oh, look. Look what we did. And then everybody else can get motivated to do it. And then it just grows like wildfire.
Interesting, you know, everybody's got a rock garden on their front lawn, and everybody's got solar panels on their roof, and so nobody's using water, and nobody's using electricity.
And, um, so we're not having hydroelectric dams that are, um, causing silt buildup and intruding on the environment and costing money, and we're not.
Um, and we could maybe even use, you know, the extra energy that we have to fuel the electricity in our cars, our electric-powered cars.
So, next time I need a job, I'm going to open up a company where I put rock gardens in people's front houses, and I install solar panels on people's houses.
But, how do you start that?
Maybe you should put solar panels on your own house.
Um, my mom has a townhouse.
So, she would have to, if I was to do that, I would have to get permission from the Homeowners Association.
So, there you go.
Then you get political experience.
And if people have political experience,
Trying to get people to agree to put, let them put solar panels on their houses and rock gardens in front of their houses,
Then they can use that political experience to get, to get involved in being politically active with other issues as well.
And vote.
And everybody wants to, should be more politically active, right?
So, um, that's my other one.
Uh, solar panels on people's houses and new industry.
